title: MANGA NGE Different Third Impacts Not in the manga
display_title: MANGA NGE Different Third Impacts Not in the manga
wikitext:
[]: [NGE] Different Third Impacts? Not in the manga.             Quote Post by Otaprince » Fri Sep 08, 2006 8:20 am as my latest thread in Eva Discussion made clear, there's still widespread confusion and debate over whether there's two types of Third Impact-- one by angels (humanity just dies), and one by humans (humanity evolves into one LCL sea of thought). It will never be resolved because the anime does not make it clear. HOWever, in the latest manga stage, 73, Sadamoto explicitly shows that there's only one type of Third Impact. Kaworu sez, "Let me tell you something else interesting. Even if Third Impact occurs, people will not simply perish. They will be reborn in a new form - merged into one as a single life form." Interesting. I think the "different third impact" theory works makes much more sense to the plot of NGE, though-- otherwise, why should Genou and SEELE fight the Angels?

[]:             Quote Post by The Goose » Tue Jan 23, 2007 6:01 pm The Angels didn't do it so that humans could become one. They did it, so that all humans would die. The reason why Gendo fought the angels was so that the world wouldn't end. The difference between the angels and Kaworu is that Kaworu was employed under Seele. He did what Seele wanted, instead of what the Angels wanted.

[]:             Quote Post by Good_Noodle » Sun Mar 04, 2007 4:24 pm I agree with goose. Why defend against angels when they fulfill SEELE's plans?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sun Mar 04, 2007 4:58 pm Sadamoto says a lot of things in the manga. Now that it has finally reached the penultimate turn and entered Third Impact, lucid nightmare plot territory, Sadamoto is just as stuck as any regular fan fiction writer. As more stages emerge, I suspect that we will encounter more and more of quite divergent manga interpretations of Third Impact. I seriously doubt it will still be an allegory for Shinji's suicide in the manga. That alone is a huge divergence in itself.

[]:             Quote Post by Fearthebait » Sun Mar 04, 2007 7:51 pm you can never really know, as with other points in the manga he might justify shinjis mind explosion part.

[]:             Quote Post by The Goose » Tue Mar 20, 2007 8:03 pm Actually I'm confused with how Sadamoto will explain Gendo's plan in the manga. During Stage 71, Gendo swallows Adam's body into his own. In the anime Gendo just put Adam's body in his hand. Why does he swallow it in the manga? The only thing I can think of is so that his whole body becomes Adam. Also in Stage 75, Gendo says that even if Kaworu had reached Lilith, Third Impact would have been incomplete, so does that mean for Gendo's 3I to take affect he has to have the Lance to complete it?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Mar 20, 2007 8:34 pm The Goose wrote: The only thing I can think of is so that his whole body becomes Adam.

[]:             Quote Post by Fearthebait » Tue Mar 20, 2007 10:46 pm Or maybe he just has a good metabolism!

[]:             Quote Post by Ayana-Me » Tue Apr 17, 2007 7:25 pm The Goose wrote: Actually I'm confused with how Sadamoto will explain Gendo's plan in the manga. During Stage 71, Gendo swallows Adam's body into his own. In the anime Gendo just put Adam's body in his hand. Why does he swallow it in the manga? The only thing I can think of is so that his whole body becomes Adam.
